title: Document trait default method contracts with doc comments
description:
## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found 8 trait default methods that return trivial values. All are architecturally correct but several lack doc comments explaining WHY the default is what it is and WHEN implementors should override.

Prerequisite beads completed:
- frankentorch-jhi (CLOSED): Changed Optimizer::set_momentum to return Result
- frankentorch-tk5 (CLOSED): Added module_type to ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported, using std::any::type_name::<Self>() in the trait default

## Affected methods (8 total)

### Module trait (ft-nn/src/lib.rs)
Current state (verified 2026-04-03):

1. `named_parameters_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return this module's own named parameters (non-recursive)." + explanation about containers
   - NEEDS: "# Default behavior" and "# When to override" sections

2. `named_parameter_slots_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return dynamically-registered own parameters, including optional slots."
   - NEEDS: default/override guidance

3. `named_buffer_slots_own()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return own registered buffers, including persistence metadata."
   - NEEDS: default/override guidance

4. `named_children()` -> Vec::new()
   - Has doc: "Return direct child sub-modules with their local names." + naming conventions
   - NEEDS: explicit note that leaf modules should keep the default

5. `register_parameter()` -> Err(Unsupported{module_type, operation})
   - Has doc: "Register (or replace) a named parameter slot."
   - NEEDS: note about default returning Unsupported with module_type context, and when to override

6. `register_buffer()` -> Err(Unsupported{module_type, operation})
   - Has doc: "Register (or replace) a named buffer slot."
   - NEEDS: same as register_parameter

### Optimizer trait (ft-optim/src/lib.rs)
Current state (verified 2026-04-03):

7. `get_momentum()` -> None
   - Has doc: "Return momentum if the optimizer exposes one."
   - NEEDS: note that None means the optimizer has no momentum concept, and override when momentum exists (e.g., SGD)

8. `set_momentum()` -> Err(unsupported)
   - Has doc: "Set momentum for optimizers that expose it."
   - NEEDS: note about the Result return, when to override, and that non-momentum optimizers return Err

## Doc comment enhancement template
For EACH method, ENHANCE (do not replace) the existing doc comment by appending:

```rust
///
/// # Default behavior
/// Returns [specific value]. This is correct for [category of types] because [reason].
///
/// # When to override
/// Override this when [condition]. Example: [`Linear`] overrides this to [what it does].
```

Do NOT delete or modify existing doc comment text — only append the new sections.

## Plan
1. Read ft-nn/src/lib.rs and ft-optim/src/lib.rs to find exact current line numbers
2. For each of the 8 methods, append the default/override sections to the existing doc comment
3. Verify: `rch exec -- cargo doc -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --no-deps 2>&1` — zero warnings
4. Verify: `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn -p ft-optim -- -D warnings` — clean
5. Verify: `git diff` shows ONLY doc comment changes, zero logic modifications

## Testing requirements

### Automated verification
- `rch exec -- cargo doc -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --no-deps` — zero warnings
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn -p ft-optim` — all existing tests pass (doc changes can't break tests, but verify)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn -p ft-optim --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean

### Manual verification (implementing agent must confirm)
- [ ] All 8 methods have "# Default behavior" section
- [ ] All 8 methods have "# When to override" section
- [ ] No method signature or logic was changed
- [ ] Existing doc text was preserved (only appended to)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 8 methods have enhanced doc comments with default behavior + override guidance
- cargo doc clean
- cargo test clean
- cargo clippy clean
- git diff shows only comment additions

## Dependencies
- frankentorch-tk5 (CLOSED) — added module_type to Unsupported variant, docs must reflect this
- frankentorch-jhi (CLOSED) — changed set_momentum signature, docs must reflect Result return

## Reasoning
Future auditors (human or AI) encountering Vec::new() defaults may incorrectly flag them as stubs. Explicit "# Default behavior" sections prevent this and serve as self-documenting contracts.
close_reason: Expanded default-trait docs for ft-nn Module traversal/registration hooks and ft-optim momentum hooks, then verified cargo doc/check/clippy via rch

title: porting: add tensor_softshrink + tensor_hardshrink for torch parity
description:
Two missing activations:
  - softshrink(x, lambd): x - lambd if x > lambd, x + lambd if x < -lambd, else 0. The proximal operator of L1 norm; used in ISTA/FISTA/sparse coding.
  - hardshrink(x, lambd): x if |x| > lambd, else 0.
Both compose via tensor_gt/lt + tensor_where; autograd-aware. Derivative is 1 outside [-lambd, lambd] and 0 inside (the where masks gradient correctly).
close_reason: Added tensor_softshrink and tensor_hardshrink in ft-api with parameterized lambd. softshrink: composes via tensor_gt + tensor_lt + chained tensor_where + tensor_sub/add (mutually exclusive masks for the two outside regions). hardshrink: tensor_abs + tensor_gt + tensor_where. Both fail-loud on negative lambd. Autograd-correct: gradient is 1 outside [-lambd, lambd] and 0 inside (the where routes the inside region to no-grad zeros). 5 unit tests cover known values, gradient regression, boundary handling, and negative-lambd rejection. ft-api now at 1307 lib tests.

title: Pre-existing test failure: custom_function_inside_no_grad_does_not_record_on_tape
description:
Pre-existing failure from commit ec29fb5 (feat(autograd,conformance): add custom function grad-disable tests). Test asserts s.tensor_grad_fn(y).unwrap().is_none() but the assertion fails. This is unrelated to gradient checkpointing or mixed precision work — verified by stashing recent changes and reproducing the failure.

Test location: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:26666

The test creates a tensor_apply_function inside no_grad context and expects:
1. tensor_requires_grad(y) returns false ✓ (this part passes)
2. tensor_grad_fn(y) returns None ✗ (this part fails)

The fix likely requires ensuring tensor_apply_function records the grad_fn as None when grad is disabled in the parent session.

title: tensor_cdist severs autograd; broadcastable composition for finite p > 0
description:
tensor_cdist at lib.rs:4230 computes pairwise L_p distances between two sets of vectors. For finite p > 0 the math is composable through autograd-aware primitives via broadcasted subtraction.

Strategy for shape [P, M] x [R, M]:
  x1_b = unsqueeze(x1, 1)    → [P, 1, M]
  x2_b = unsqueeze(x2, 0)    → [1, R, M]
  diff = x1_b - x2_b         → [P, R, M] (broadcast)
  d    = (sum_k |diff[k]|^p)^(1/p)

For batched [B, P, M] x [B, R, M] the same pattern works at dims 2 and 1.

Sister of frankentorch-0i8u (pdist). p == 0 / p == +inf still fail-loud on requires_grad.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_cdist through unsqueeze + expand + sub + abs + pow + sum_dim + pow primitives for both 2-D and 3-D batched inputs. The broadcasted subtraction handles both [P, M] x [R, M] and [B, P, M] x [B, R, M] cases. p == 0 / p == +inf still fail-loud on requires_grad to make autograd severance explicit (deferred to a follow-up — same pattern as pdist 0i8u). Added cdist_l2_propagates_gradient_through_both_inputs regression test (single-pair x1=[[0,0]], x2=[[3,4]], p=2 → grad_x1=[-3/5,-4/5], grad_x2=[3/5,4/5]). ft-api lib tests: 1170.

title: [parity-gap] ft-api missing tensor_addcmul / tensor_addcdiv
description: Deep-review (StormyBirch 2026-05-20): ft-api exposes 398 tensor_* ops but has no tensor_addcmul or tensor_addcdiv. torch.addcmul(input, t1, t2, value=1) = input + value*t1*t2 and torch.addcdiv(input, t1, t2, value=1) = input + value*t1*t2.recip() are public torch ops (also in-place addcmul_/addcdiv_) used widely in optimizer update rules and elementwise fused math. Both are trivially composable through existing autograd-aware primitives (tensor_mul/tensor_div/tensor_add + a scalar) so they can be added to ft-api without kernel/dispatch work, like the var/std correction rescale. Verified absent: no 'addcmul'/'addcdiv' anywhere in crates/ft-api or ft-optim; 0 hits in issues.jsonl. Acceptance: tensor_addcmul/tensor_addcdiv (+ tensor_addcmul_/tensor_addcdiv_ if in-place is wanted), autograd-correct, with focused ft-api regressions and a torch 2.12 differential check.
close_reason: Implemented tensor_addcmul/tensor_addcdiv in ft-api, composed through autograd-aware primitives. Verified vs torch 2.12. Commit 1c13f3d.

title: Enrich Module register_parameter/register_buffer errors with module type context
description:
## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found that Module::register_parameter() and Module::register_buffer() default implementations (ft-nn/src/lib.rs) return `Err(ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported { operation: "register_parameter" })`.

The error tells you WHAT operation failed but not WHICH module rejected it. When debugging a deep Sequential model with 50+ layers, "register_parameter unsupported" gives no clue about which layer is the problem.

## Current error type
```rust
pub enum ModuleRegistrationError {
    InvalidName { kind: &'static str },
    NameConflict { name: String },
    Unsupported { operation: &'static str },
}
```

## Design decision: How to get the module type name in a trait default

The trait default method signature is `fn register_parameter(&mut self, ...)`. We CANNOT call `std::any::type_name::<Self>()` because the default method has `Self: ?Sized` (it's on `dyn Module`).

**Solution**: Add a new required method to the Module trait:
```rust
fn module_type_name(&self) -> &'static str;
```
This is cheap (one &'static str per impl), gives correct type info even through dyn dispatch, and follows the same pattern PyTorch uses with `_get_name()`.

Each module implementation returns its name: "Linear", "Conv2d", "Sequential", etc.

The default for register_parameter/register_buffer then becomes:
```rust
fn register_parameter(...) -> Result<(), ModuleRegistrationError> {
    Err(ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported {
        operation: "register_parameter",
        module_type: self.module_type_name(),
    })
}
```

## Plan
1. Add `module_type_name(&self) -> &'static str` to the Module trait (no default — every impl must provide it)
2. Add `module_type: &'static str` field to `ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported`
3. Update the Display impl to print "{module_type} does not support {operation}"
4. Update default register_parameter/register_buffer to include module_type
5. Add `module_type_name()` to ALL existing Module implementations (~40+ structs):
   - Linear -> "Linear", Conv1d -> "Conv1d", Sequential -> "Sequential", etc.
6. Update any tests that construct or match ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported

## Testing requirements

### Unit tests (in ft-nn #[cfg(test)])

#### test_unsupported_register_includes_module_type
- Call register_parameter on a module that doesn't override it (e.g., ReLU)
- Assert the error message contains "ReLU"
- Assert the error message contains "register_parameter"

#### test_unsupported_register_buffer_includes_module_type
- Same for register_buffer on a leaf module

#### test_all_modules_have_type_name
- For each major module type (Linear, Conv1d, Conv2d, LSTM, Sequential, etc.):
  - Construct an instance
  - Assert module_type_name() returns a non-empty string
  - Assert module_type_name() does NOT contain "Module" alone (should be specific)

#### test_module_type_name_matches_struct_name
- For a selection of modules, verify that module_type_name() matches the Rust struct name
- E.g., Linear::module_type_name() == "Linear"

#### test_sequential_register_parameter_succeeds
- Verify that Sequential (which DOES support register_parameter) still works correctly
- Construct a Sequential, register a parameter, verify it appears in named_parameter_slots_own()

### E2E validation
- Run `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn` — all existing + new tests pass
- Run `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean

## Acceptance Criteria
- Error messages include the module type name (e.g., "ReLU does not support register_parameter")
- ALL Module implementations have module_type_name()
- 5+ new tests covering the enriched error paths
- Existing tests still pass
- Clippy clean

## Risk
Medium — this adds a required trait method to ~40 implementations. Use grep to find all `impl Module for` blocks. Each just needs one line added. Can be parallelized with subagents.

## Dependencies
None — isolated change within ft-nn.
close_reason: ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported now carries module_type context, default Module registration methods report std::any::type_name::<Self>(), and ft-nn test plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch

title: tensor_interpolate bicubic mode autograd via 16-corner cubic Hermite gather
description:
After frankentorch-3t1t added linear/bilinear/trilinear autograd, bicubic still fails loud. PyTorch's bicubic interpolation uses cubic Hermite spline weights for 4 source pixels per axis (16 total in 2-D). Compose via 16 tensor_index_select calls + non-grad weight tensors + accumulating tensor_add — same partition-of-unity weighted blend as bilinear, just with 16 corners and cubic-Hermite weights.

Per-axis cubic Hermite weights (PyTorch's bicubic_convolution variant, A=-0.75):
  w0 = A*t*(t-1)^2
  w1 = (A+2)*t^3 - (A+3)*t^2 + 1
  w2 = -(A+2)*t^3 + (2A+3)*t^2 - A*t
  w3 = -A*t^2*(t-1)
where t = frac(src). Source pixels are at floor(src) - 1, floor(src), floor(src)+1, floor(src)+2 (clamped).

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_interpolate (line 14674).

title: pack_padded_sequence and pad_packed_sequence sever autograd
description:
Both pack_padded_sequence and pad_packed_sequence in ft-nn extract tensor_values from input, manually permute/copy to a new layout, and rebuild via session.tensor_variable(_, _, false). This silently severs autograd through every RNN/LSTM/GRU training pipeline that uses torch.nn.utils.rnn.pack_padded_sequence or pad_packed_sequence (which is most of them).

Fix:
- pack_padded_sequence: reshape input to [T*B, F], compute the flat index list (for each timestep t with batch_size bs[t], emit t*B + sorted_indices[s] for s in 0..bs[t]), then call tensor_index_select to gather the rows. Shape back if needed.
- pad_packed_sequence: invert the operation. The packed data has shape [total_elements/F, F]; build a [B*T, F] output by scatter-then-pad. Where each packed row k corresponds to (t, sorted_indices[s]), set output row at orig_batch * T + t. Use tensor_scatter (autograd-aware) and a non-grad fill tensor for padding_value.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs pack_padded_sequence (line 13364), pad_packed_sequence (line 13490).

title: [perf][bug] ft-api: fused f32 batch_norm (stats+apply) + fix f32->f64 upcast
description: Last norm in the f32-no-grad audit vein (after layer/rms/group: pn8v/xpvf/r2hi, ~19-21x each + dtype-upcast fix). try_fused_batch_norm (ft-api ~19989) is f64-only; f32 batch_norm2d/1d/3d fall through to the permute+op-graph path which also upcasts f32->f64. MORE INVOLVED than the single-kernel norms: needs ft_kernel_cpu::batch_norm_stats_f32 + batch_norm_apply_f32 (mirrors of the f64 kernels ~19928), AND the running_mean/running_var momentum updates ((1-m)*r + m*stat, var with Bessel sample_count/(sample_count-1)) done in f32, across BOTH training (compute+update stats) and eval (use running stats) branches in try_fused_batch_norm's no-grad block (20014-20075). Isomorphism: f32 fused vs op-graph within f32 tol (op-graph returns F64 -> read via tensor_values; mirror functional_batch_norm2d_fused_matches_op_graph test). Bench: add group_norm-style nograd_f32 case (N=32,C=256,28x28). Expected ~17x (BatchNorm op-graph does 2 full-tensor permutes + ~10 nodes). Covers batch_norm1d/3d via the shared helper. Use the awk first-hunk patch isolation for the shared kernel file (active eigh peer); do NOT git --amend.
close_reason: SHIPPED: fused f32 batch_norm (stats_f32+apply_f32 + f32 momentum running-stat updates, train+eval) + fixed f32->f64 upcast. A/B batch_norm/nograd_f32_*_32x256x28x28: train 604.55->14.41ms=41.9x, eval 594.28->13.70ms=43.4x. Isomorphism within f32 tol (output+stats, train+eval). Norm family f32 now COMPLETE (layer/rms/group/batch+instance).

title: soft_margin_loss severs autograd; composable through neg/mul/relu/abs/log1p/exp
description:
soft_margin_loss at lib.rs:10423 computes log(1 + exp(-t*x)) per element in plain f64, builds the per-element loss as a requires_grad=false leaf, then takes tensor_mean — but the gradient can no longer reach input or target because the leaf has severed the tape.

Math: with z = -t*x,
   log1p(exp(z)) = max(0, z) + log1p(exp(-|z|))
                 = relu(z) + log1p(exp(-abs(z)))

Composable through autograd-aware primitives:
  z = tensor_mul(tensor_neg(target), input)
  loss_per_elem = tensor_add(tensor_relu(z), tensor_log1p(tensor_exp(tensor_neg(tensor_abs(z)))))
  loss = tensor_mean(loss_per_elem)

Same numerical-stability form, same per-element math, but with the autograd tape preserved.

Sister of frankentorch-ilwr / 4liu (other severed loss functions). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed soft_margin_loss through neg/mul/relu/abs/log1p/exp/add/mean primitives — same numerical-stability form (relu(z) + log1p(exp(-|z|)) where z = -t*x) but with the autograd tape preserved. Added soft_margin_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (input=[1,-1], target=[1,-1] under mean loss → analytical [-1/(2(1+e)), 1/(2(1+e))]). ft-api lib tests: 1153.

title: Fix clippy approx_constant correctness errors in Bessel asymptotics
description: cargo clippy -p ft-api fails to compile (8 deny-by-default approx_constant correctness errors) because bessel_j0/j1/y0/y1_scalar use truncated literals 0.636619772 (2/pi) and 0.785398163397448 (pi/4) in their A&S 9.4.x asymptotic/small-x forms. Replace with std::f64::consts::FRAC_2_PI and FRAC_PI_4. More accurate (2/pi literal was truncated to 9 sig-figs, delta 3.7e-10; pi/4 delta 3.3e-16), well inside the rational approximations' intrinsic ~1e-7 error, so Bessel outputs are preserved to better than the approximation's own accuracy. Unblocks cargo clippy -p ft-api --lib (now compiles; only pre-existing pedantic warnings remain). No golden value tests assert J/Y outputs; ft-api lib tests pass.
close_reason: Replaced truncated 2/pi and pi/4 literals in bessel_j0/j1/y0/y1_scalar with std::f64::consts::FRAC_2_PI / FRAC_PI_4. cargo clippy -p ft-api --lib now compiles (0 approx_constant correctness errors, was 8). Bessel outputs preserved to better than approximation accuracy; ft-api bessel/nanvar tests pass.

Acceptance: add ReLU6 module in ft-nn, implement via autograd-aware clamp to [0, 6], expose no parameters, add forward/shape/no-parameter/backward tests, and validate via rch ft-nn gates plus UBS.

title: Severed-autograd in linalg ops: cholesky_solve, linalg_inv, triangular_solve, slogdet, det
description:
After closing matrix_norm (frankentorch-i4rd), several other linalg ops in ft-api still extract values, run a kernel, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf — same severed-autograd pattern fixed under frankentorch-1tax for cov / corrcoef / mode etc.

Affected ops:
  - tensor_linalg_inv (lib.rs:11485) — d(A^{-1})/dA: grad_A = -A^{-T} grad_out A^{-T}. Composable via matmul + transpose + neg, with A^{-1} saved.
  - tensor_triangular_solve (lib.rs:11500) — d X / dB = A^{-T}, dX/dA contracted with grad gives an outer-product term masked to the triangular pattern.
  - tensor_cholesky_solve (lib.rs:11453) — chains through cholesky factor; gradient flows through the factor's triangular_solve calls.
  - tensor_linalg_det / tensor_linalg_slogdet — Jacobi formula: d log|det A| / dA = A^{-T}; det case: d det A / dA = det(A) * A^{-T}.

PyTorch's torch.linalg.{inv,solve_triangular,cholesky_solve,det,slogdet} are all differentiable. Surfaced during a /reality-check pass scanning for  and  patterns in linalg op bodies.

Each op gets its own child bead. Priority order based on usage frequency: linalg_inv → triangular_solve → cholesky_solve → det/slogdet.
notes:
All TensorNodeId-returning linalg ops now have autograd:
  - tensor_linalg_inv         frq7 (d364f8e)  — Y^T grad Y^T backward
  - tensor_triangular_solve   skpl (163365f)  — substitution backward
  - tensor_cholesky_solve     this commit     — composes through triangular_solve
  
The det / slogdet ops were originally listed as children but they currently return raw f64 (not TensorNodeId), so they're outside the autograd graph entirely. Adding autograd to them requires an API change (return TensorNodeId), which is a separate concern from the severed-autograd fix this umbrella tracked. Filed as a separate concern if needed.

title: Add E2E training test for BatchNorm1d + Linear regressor

title: ft-api: f16/bf16 dtype gaps across movement/select ops (upcast or error)
description:
Half-precision (f16/bf16) sweep found many ops that error or upcast (torch supports f16 for all):
UPCAST (wrong dtype): masked_select -> F64; cat -> F32; stack -> F32.
HARD-ERROR ('requires f32 or f64' / UnsupportedDType): gather, where, diagonal, tril, triu, nan_to_num, scatter_add, index_add (and cummax transitively via index_select).
roll/flip/repeat_interleave/unfold/clamp/norm/dist/logsumexp/sort/topk/quantile already preserve f16. Fix in classes: (1) upcast group = dtype preservation (no kernel math needed); (2) error group = relax dtype guard / upcast-compute-narrow per op (many are pure select/copy so dtype-agnostic). Probe: probe_f16_op_gaps.
close_reason: All MOVEMENT/SELECT/PREDICATE f16/bf16 dtype gaps fixed across 8 bit-exact commits: cat/stack 3f7f7bc7, index_select+take+cummax a5332e3c, diagonal/tril/triu f2046f93, masked_select+gather 0bea119a, where 0d2bb62b, comparisons+isnan/isinf 452f41c0, nan_to_num c2c839b3. Each upcasts to f64/f32 (lossless for half), computes, narrows the mask/gathered result back to the input half dtype; all bit-exact (pure copy/select/predicate, no accumulation) + grad-preserving + f32/f64 no-regression, with per-op golden-value tests. REMAINING scatter_add/index_add (arithmetic accumulation) split out to a new P3 bead — they need a torch-CPU f16-accumulation parity decision before implementing (deferred, not mechanical).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: cat/stack SHIPPED 3f7f7bc7 (join dispatch now narrows back to shared f16/bf16, bit-exact since join is a pure copy). REMAINING two groups: (1) masked_select upcast->F64 and (2) hard-error group gather/where/diagonal/tril/triu/nan_to_num/scatter_add/index_add ('requires f32 or f64'). KEY DEPENDENCY discovered: index_select rejects f16 ('index_select requires f32 or f64 tensors'), which transitively blocks cummax(f16), take(f16), masked_select(f16) — so the highest-leverage NEXT lever is adding f16/bf16 support to index_select (ft-autograd, upcast-gather-narrow or native half gather), which unblocks that whole cluster at once. The pure select/copy ops (diagonal/tril/triu) reject f16 only via an over-strict dtype guard (no arithmetic needed) and are individually cheap. nan_to_num/where/scatter_add/index_add need upcast-compute-narrow per op.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: index_select f16/bf16 SHIPPED a5332e3c (pure-gather: upcast to f64, gather, narrow back — bit-exact). This UNBLOCKS take(f16) and cummax/cummin(f16) which now keep dtype (verified). REMAINING: masked_select (still uses tensor_apply_function -> upcasts to F64; now fixable by routing through index_select gather like kthvalue/cummax); gather (own f16 kernel guard); diagonal/tril/triu (pure select, over-strict 'requires f32 or f64' guard — cheap to relax); where/nan_to_num/scatter_add/index_add (need upcast-compute-narrow per op). Next lever: masked_select via index_select gather, then the diagonal/tril/triu guard relaxation batch.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: diagonal/tril/triu SHIPPED f2046f93 (diagonal: relax guard + unify empty via the now-f16 index_select gather; tril/triu: unified apply_function + to_dtype narrow — fixes f32 AND f16/bf16, keeps explicit-zero NaN/inf semantics). REMAINING: masked_select (still tensor_apply_function -> F64; fixable via index_select gather now), gather (own f16 kernel guard 'gather requires f32 or f64'), where/nan_to_num/scatter_add/index_add (need upcast-compute-narrow per op). Progress so far: cat/stack 3f7f7bc7, index_select+take+cummax a5332e3c, diagonal/tril/triu f2046f93.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: masked_select + gather SHIPPED 0bea119a (gather: f64-upcast-gather-narrow arm like index_select; masked_select: rewritten to gather kept elements via index_select -> preserves dtype incl f16/bf16 AND fixed a bonus f32->F64 upcast). REMAINING (final batch): where, nan_to_num, scatter_add, index_add — these do arithmetic/conditional selection and need upcast-compute-narrow per op. After that batch u78p closes. Progress: cat/stack 3f7f7bc7, index_select+take+cummax a5332e3c, diagonal/tril/triu f2046f93, masked_select+gather 0bea119a.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: where SHIPPED 0d2bb62b (catch-all was upcasting all-f16/bf16 select to f64; routed through reduction_values_storage — pure selection so lossless, bit-exact). REMAINING (both have dependencies/risk, NOT pure-copy): (1) nan_to_num composes via tensor_eq which rejects f16 ('comparison requires matching f32 or f64 dtypes') — BLOCKED until tensor_comparison gets f16 support (clean upcast-to-f64 compare, bit-exact since f16->f64 lossless; that is the next clean lever and unblocks nan_to_num). (2) scatter_add/index_add do arithmetic ACCUMULATION — an upcast-to-f64-accumulate-narrow would NOT be bit-exact to torch's f16 per-add rounding (torch may use fp32 acc internally); needs a parity decision before implementing, so DEFERRED. Movement/select half-dtype gaps are otherwise closed: cat/stack, index_select/take/cummax, diagonal/tril/triu, masked_select/gather, where.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: comparisons (eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge) + isnan/isinf SHIPPED 452f41c0 (value-only predicates: upcast to f64, compute, narrow 0/1 mask back to operand half dtype — bit-exact). This UNBLOCKS nan_to_num(f16): its only remaining blocker was tensor_eq+isnan rejecting f16. REMAINING: (1) nan_to_num — add F16|BF16 to its matched arm; the composed path (full_like + eq + where + isnan) now all support f16, BUT the default posinf/neginf must use the HALF dtype's max/min (f16 MAX=65504, NOT f64::MAX — passing f64::MAX to full_like(f16) would round to +inf and break the replacement). Next clean lever. (2) scatter_add/index_add still DEFERRED (arithmetic accumulation, bit-exactness vs torch f16 per-add rounding needs a parity decision). Progress: cat/stack, index_select/take/cummax, diagonal/tril/triu, masked_select/gather, where, comparisons+isnan/isinf.

title: ft-autograd: enable double-backward (create_graph) for VarDim and StdDim
description: backward_create_graph fail-loud errored for VarDim and StdDim, blocking second-order autograd through variance/std reductions. VarDim: grad=grad_slice*2*(x-mean)/(n-1); build centered=x-broadcast(mean_dim(x)) via a differentiable MeanDim node so the second backward routes d(mean)/dx, recovering the exact per-slice Hessian (2/c)(I-1/n). StdDim: grad=grad_slice*(x-mean)/((n-1)*std); reuse the StdDim output node as the per-slice cg_div divisor (times constant correction) so the second backward routes d(std)/dx. Added cg_broadcast_along_dim + cg_centered_along_dim helpers (reshape-to-size-1 + cg_expand). Both shift-invariant (H.1=0) so tests probe H.w via the FD helper assert_softmax_like_second_derivative.

title: ft-api: scatter_add/index_add f16/bf16 (arithmetic accumulation — needs parity decision)
description: scatter_add and index_add reject f16/bf16 ('requires f32 or f64 tensors'). Unlike the movement/select ops (closed under u78p), these ACCUMULATE (out[idx] += src), so the upcast-to-f64-accumulate-then-narrow approach used for gather/index_select is NOT necessarily bit-exact to torch: torch's f16 scatter_add rounds per-add (or uses an fp32 acc_type internally). REQUIRED FIRST: determine torch CPU's exact f16 scatter_add accumulation semantics (per-add half rounding vs fp32 accumulate), then implement to match. Until that parity decision is made this stays deferred — do NOT ship a rounding-divergent accumulation. Probe: probe_f16_op_gaps (in earlier u78p worktrees).
notes: RESOLVED the parity question (219ba55c). Installed torch 2.12.0+cpu, probed CPU half accumulation over 200 random trials/dtype: scatter_add = fp32-accumulate-then-narrow (acc_type<Half>=float); index_add & index_put(accumulate=true) = per-add HALF rounding (DIFFERENT). Discriminator: 1.0 + 10*f16(0.0003) -> scatter_add 1.0029, index_add 1.0. scatter_add f16/bf16 SHIPPED (upcast->f32, f32 kernel, narrow; bit-exact golden test vs torch). index_add/index_put-accumulate split to follow-up bead (per-add-half kernel) — they keep their own gates so they stay correctly rejected for now.
close_reason: scatter_add f16/bf16 SHIPPED 219ba55c (torch-exact fp32-accumulate, verified vs torch 2.12 over 200 trials, golden test). The parity question that blocked this for 3 sessions is RESOLVED: scatter_add=fp32-acc, index_add/index_put-accumulate=per-add-half (different). index_add portion superseded by k4wr (per-add-half kernel — semantics now known, just needs implementation).

title: porting: add tensor_tanhshrink + tensor_softsign for torch parity
description:
Two simple activations missing from the functional ft-api:
  - tanhshrink(x) = x - tanh(x). Compose via tensor_tanh + tensor_sub.
  - softsign(x) = x / (1 + |x|). Compose via tensor_abs + tensor_add + tensor_div.
Both autograd-aware via existing primitives. Used in some specialized neural network architectures.
close_reason: Added tensor_tanhshrink (x - tanh(x)) and tensor_softsign (x / (1 + |x|)) as functional ft-api ops. Both compose through existing autograd-aware primitives. 4 unit tests cover known values (including odd-function check for tanhshrink), large-input asymptotic for softsign, and analytic gradient regression for both: tanh²(x) for tanhshrink and 1/(1+|x|)² for softsign. ft-api now at 1302 lib tests.

title: tensor_scatter_reduce mean mode autograd via scatter_add + count + div
description:
After frankentorch-dhm4 added autograd to scatter_reduce sum mode, the mean mode still fails loud. Compose mean via two scatter_adds:
1. count_tensor = scatter_add(zeros, dim, index, ones_like_src) — number of hits per output position
2. sum_tensor = scatter_add(input, dim, index, src) — input + sum of mapped src
3. divisor = count_tensor + 1 (the +1 accounts for the original input)
4. output = sum_tensor / divisor

For positions not hit (count=0), divisor=1 → output = input/1 = input, matching include_self=True semantics.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs tensor_scatter_reduce (line 16249).

title: ft-api: modified Bessel I-family upgraded to Cephes f64 precision
description: i0/i0e/i1/i1e used single-precision Abramowitz-Stegun polynomials (~1.6e-7 rel) — an ~8-orders regression vs torch (Cephes f64). Replaced with pure-Rust Cephes Chebyshev expansions (chbevl + I0/I1 A/B coeff arrays) accurate to ~1 ULP, bit-aligning with torch. Routes all 8 scalar fns (bessel_i0/i0e/i1/i1e_scalar + i0/i1/i0e/i1e_approx) through the new core. Verified vs scipy f64 reference values. Regenerated the i0 golden fixture. frankentorch-2vxa.
close_reason: Shipped commit 10cb0e7a: modified Bessel I-family (i0/i0e/i1/i1e) upgraded from single-precision A&S to pure-Rust Cephes f64 Chebyshev expansions, ~1 ULP, bit-aligned with torch. Verified vs scipy refs, golden fixture regenerated. Pushed main+master.

title: ft-api: add tensor_log2_ in-place operation
close_reason: done

title: [parity-gap] torch.special.log_ndtr
description: torch.special.log_ndtr(input) computes log of standard Gaussian CDF. See https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/special.html#torch.special.log_ndtr
close_reason: done

title: Autograd tensor tape grows unbounded — no node free/clear (memory leak)
description: TensorTape (crates/ft-autograd/src/lib.rs:4165) pushes a TensorNode for EVERY op and never frees them. Each node owns a DenseTensor (the full tensor data). There is NO clear/truncate/reset/free API and the only handling is a / flag set after backward(!retain_graph) at ~3980 that does NOT free nodes. Consequences: (1) INFERENCE/forward-only loops leak forever — under no_grad the push still happens (only requires_grad is gated, line ~1569/1577), nodes accumulate with the tensor data; (2) TRAINING leaks across steps unless the whole session is dropped (no per-step graph free). Affects every long-running workload. Verified: grep finds zero nodes.clear/truncate/drain; node access is via 382 scattered self.nodes[id.0] sites (no central accessor). FIX (multi-turn, architectural): NodeId is a flat-arena index, so a naive clear() reuses index 0 and ALIASES stale handles -> use offset/generational indexing: add base_offset (vec_idx = id.0 - base; id.0 < base => StaleHandle error), route the 382 accesses through a single checked accessor, then clear() does base += len; nodes.clear() (also truncate the parallel gradients/gradient_nodes Vecs + drop retains_grad entries >= boundary). That enables a SAFE session.clear_autograd_graph() (inference, between requests) and truncate_to(boundary) (training, keep param leaves at low indices, free per-step graph after optimizer.step()). Mirrors the EvidenceLedger leak fix (2f820623) but harder due to live handles. Interim mitigation shipped: session-level node-count observability so consumers can monitor growth + recreate the session before OOM.
close_reason: Fixed in d831f0e0: added TensorTape::truncate_graph_to(boundary)/clear_graph() + session truncate_autograd_graph/clear_autograd_graph. Reclaims the append-only arena (nodes + persistent_grads/tensor_hooks/retains_grad) without touching the 382 access sites; purely additive (default behavior unchanged, all tests pass). Callers capture node_count() after model init then truncate after each step/request. Mechanism shipped; fully-automatic free-after-backward could be a future enhancement but the API is the correct primitive (mirrors PyTorch graph-freed-after-backward).

title: [parity-gap] ft-nn Embedding missing padding_idx and max_norm
description: EmeraldPine audit (2026-05-20). nn.Embedding (crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs:2396-2443) has no padding_idx (PyTorch zeroes that weight row and returns the zero vector + zero gradient for it) and no max_norm (PyTorch renormalizes any embedding row whose L2 norm exceeds max_norm, in-place, on forward). Both are commonly relied on by sequence models. GRU new-gate, LSTM gate order (i,f,g,o), Dropout 1/(1-p) scaling, AlphaDropout constants, Conv kaiming_uniform(a=sqrt(5)) bounds were all verified CORRECT.
close_reason: nn.Embedding gains padding_idx (zero row + zero grad via output mask) and max_norm/norm_type (in-place accessed-row renorm matching torch.embedding_renorm_). Verified vs torch 2.12. Commit ad7d07b.

title: FD gradient checks: linalg_slogdet logabsdet and linalg_inv backward
description: Companion to frankentorch-26ch (det FD check). Add finite-difference gradient checks for: (a) tensor_linalg_slogdet's logabsdet output (autograd-aware path added under pvfk; backward = (A^{-1})^T), and (b) tensor_linalg_inv's backward (Y^T grad Y^T composition added earlier under tw0y). Covers the full Jacobi-formula family of linalg backwards and locks them against future regressions.
close_reason: Added two FD gradient checks: (1) linalg_slogdet_logabsdet_backward_matches_central_finite_difference — Jacobi ∂logabsdet/∂A = (A^{-1})^T; (2) linalg_inv_backward_matches_central_finite_difference — composite ∂sum(A^{-1})/∂A via the Y^T grad Y^T identity. Both run on 2-3 well-conditioned 2x2/3x3 fixtures with eps=1e-6 and abs_tol=5e-5..5e-4. Locks the slogdet (pvfk) and inv (tw0y) backward formulas against future regressions. ft-conformance now at 150 base lib tests, 196 with --features fuzz.

title: ft-nn CELU forward severs autograd
description: Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs. Recent commits fixed SELU, Threshold, Hardtanh, Unfold, and Fold to compose through autograd-aware primitives, but CELU::forward at lines 3673-3690 still calls session.tensor_values(input), computes outputs in plain f64, and returns session.tensor_variable(output, input_shape, false). Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, call CELU::new(alpha).forward(...), reduce output with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for the CELU input will be absent/zero because the output is a fresh requires_grad=false leaf. This silently breaks training through CELU and violates the deterministic autograd contract. Fix: implement CELU via differentiable primitives, e.g. x_pos = relu(input), x_scaled = input / alpha, neg_branch = alpha * (exp(x_scaled) - 1) selected where input < 0, or an equivalent tensor_where/tensor_exp composition, and add a regression test asserting CELU input gradients match the analytical derivative.
close_reason: CELU::forward now composes through tensor_where(input >= 0, input, alpha * (exp(input/alpha) - 1)) using tensor_div / tensor_mul / tensor_exp / tensor_sub primitives — all autograd-aware. Added celu_propagates_gradients regression test asserting analytical d CELU/dx (1 for x>=0, exp(x/alpha) for x<0) plus celu_rejects_zero_alpha guard.

title: Fix gaussian_nll_loss autograd via tensor primitives [child of 2med]
description:
gaussian_nll_loss at lib.rs:10398 computes:
  loss_i = 0.5 * (log(var_clamped_i) + (input_i - target_i)^2 / var_clamped_i)
plus optional 0.5 * log(2pi) constant.

Composable through tensor_clamp_min + tensor_log + tensor_sub + tensor_mul + tensor_div + scalar add for the optional constant. All three of input / target / var are differentiable in PyTorch's gaussian_nll_loss.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed gaussian_nll_loss through clamp_min/log/sub/mul/div/mean primitives. Added gaussian_nll_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input_and_var test (input=2, target=1, var=4 → grad_input = 0.25, grad_var = 0.09375).

title: CPU round kernels use half-away instead of PyTorch half-even
description: FrankenTorch CPU round parity bug. PyTorch torch.round uses round-half-to-even for ties, including round(2.5) -> 2 and round(-0.5) -> -0.0. The ft-kernel-cpu scalar and f64/f32 tensor round paths used Rust f64::round/f32::round, which round ties away from zero, so values like [-2.5, -0.5, 0.5, 2.5] diverged from PyTorch-observable semantics. Affected file: crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs. Fix: use round_ties_even for scalar, f64 tensor, and f32 tensor round kernels, and add explicit tie-even tests covering negative zero.
close_reason: Implemented ft-kernel-cpu round_ties_even parity for scalar/f64/f32 paths and added tie-even tests.

title: Add metamorphic property tests: min≤max, argmax of x equals argmin of -x, scatter+gather round-trip
description:
Three more metamorphic property tests:
1. fuzz_metamorphic_min_le_max — tensor_min_dim and tensor_max_dim outputs satisfy min[i] ≤ max[i] elementwise
2. fuzz_metamorphic_argmax_equals_argmin_of_neg — argmax(x, 0) bit-exactly equals argmin(neg(x), 0)
3. fuzz_metamorphic_gather_scatter_round_trip — scatter(zeros, dim, idx, gather(x, dim, idx)) at indexed positions matches x

title: Audit UBS warnings in ft-api (unwrap/assert hotspots)
description: UBS scan of crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs reported large counts of unwrap/expect and assert macros plus mutex lock expects. Triage which are test-only vs runtime paths, and replace reachable unwrap/assert with explicit error handling where needed.
close_reason: Replaced runtime unwrap/expect paths in ft-api with explicit errors + overflow checks

title: ft-api: add tensor_split
description: Add tensor_split function to ft-api. PyTorch's tensor_split splits a tensor into multiple sub-tensors, all of which are views of input, along dimension dim according to the indices or number of sections specified.

title: Implement Unfold and Fold modules in ft-nn
description: Add nn.Unfold (im2col) and nn.Fold (col2im) modules. Critical for depthwise-separable convolutions and some attention patterns.
close_reason: Implemented nn.Unfold (im2col) and nn.Fold (col2im) with padding/stride/dilation. 4 tests including roundtrip.

title: ft-api tensor_nan_to_num silently severs autograd on tracked input
description: tensor_nan_to_num at crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:13938+ extracts values via self.tensor_tape.values(input)?, replaces NaN/±inf with constants, and creates a new leaf via self.tensor_tape.leaf(values, shape, false). No requires_grad guard. If input.requires_grad == true, the user expects gradients to flow through non-NaN/inf positions (NaN/inf positions go to zero — those values were replaced by constants, so backward should be 0 there). Current behavior silently produces requires_grad=false leaf, breaking backward through any model that uses nan_to_num for masked-loss or numerical-stability purposes. Same severed-autograd pattern as the cov / corrcoef / mode / quantile family fixed under frankentorch-1tax. Fix by composing through tensor_isnan + tensor_isinf + tensor_where so gradients flow correctly to non-replaced positions.
close_reason: Fixed tensor_nan_to_num severed-autograd on F64. Replaced the values-extract-and-rebuild-leaf path with autograd-aware composition: tensor_eq (vs +inf and -inf constant tensors) + tensor_isnan + chained tensor_where calls. Order matters: replace +inf, -inf first (NaN passes through eq checks per IEEE 754), then NaN — final isnan catches it. Gradients now flow through finite, non-NaN positions and are zero at replaced positions, matching torch.nan_to_num's backward semantics. F32 path: added requires_grad guard that fails loud (tensor_where dispatches in F64 only, so the F32 autograd composition would type-mismatch). 2 new regression tests in ft-api: nan_to_num_propagates_gradient_through_finite_positions, nan_to_num_f32_with_requires_grad_fails_loud. ft-api now at 1217 lib tests.

title: ft-api: linalg_eigh full autograd backward (eigenvalues + eigenvectors)
description: eigh failed loud on requires_grad (multi-output was thought to need a tape primitive). Solved WITHOUT one: build two independent single-output apply_function nodes from the same input; the engine sums their grad_A contributions (accumulate is +=). Eigenvalue node: grad_A=V diag(grad_l) V^T. Eigenvector node: grad_A=sym(V (F . (V^T grad_V)) V^T), F_ab=1/(l_b-l_a). Both FD-verified (eigenvector loss is gauge-invariant sum_k (c^T v_k)^2). frankentorch-rl4g.
close_reason: Shipped commit 34cfd621: full eigh autograd (eigenvalues + eigenvectors) via two independent apply_function nodes that sum grad_A — NO multi-output tape needed. Both FD-verified, eigenvector loss gauge-invariant. Pushed main+master. Unblocks svd/qr via same pattern.

title: Align conformance smoke coverage and green-scope ledger with existing fixture families

title: [perf] ft-runtime: store static policy evidence summaries without allocation

RCH Criterion on ts1 for runtime_policy_evidence/new_and_switch_1024 baseline: [91.932 us 92.719 us 93.680 us]. One lever: change evidence summaries to a static-or-owned representation, preserving entry order, timestamp capture per record, kind values, rendered summary text, dynamic evidence ownership, and golden sha256. title: [perf][fallback] ft-serialize: hoist fixed-width byte reads
description: Profile-backed fallback target because br ready is empty and active perf beads are owned by other agents. RCH Criterion baseline on vmi1149989 for native_state_dict/decode_many_small_f64_1024x4: [246.90 us 262.42 us 285.89 us]. Hotspot surface is native state-dict decode of many tiny tensor records; every key/shape header calls read_fixed_bytes, which already bounds-checks the exact slice before routing through bytes_to_array/try_into. One lever: replace that post-check conversion with a stack array copy after the proven length check. Preserve all error ordering/reasons, dtype/shape/key order, duplicate-key behavior, floating-point bits, no RNG/tie behavior. Keep only if rebench clears Score>=2.0 and existing ft_serialize_decode_pass19 golden sha256 verifies.

title: Eliminate ft-conformance dead_code warnings blocking clippy
close_reason: Validated fixture metadata and removed ft-conformance dead_code warnings

title: Add fail-loud guards on bernoulli/poisson sampling for requires_grad input
description:
tensor_bernoulli (line 2137) and tensor_poisson (line 2169) silently sever autograd by extracting tensor_values, drawing samples in plain f64, and rebuilding non-grad leaves. Both sampling operations are non-differentiable (PyTorch users use rsample for differentiable variants). Match the pattern used for L0 vector_norm and other intrinsically non-differentiable ops by adding fail-loud guards on requires_grad input.

Affects: crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs bernoulli (line 2137), poisson (line 2169).

title: Validate performance SLOs from spec against actual measurements
description:
COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §17 defines 8 performance budgets that have NEVER been measured:

| Path | Workload | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| elementwise forward | 100M elements | p95 <= 200 ms |
| backward pass | representative autograd graphs | p95 <= 1.35x forward baseline |
| matmul core | medium dense matrices | p95 <= 240 ms |
| optimizer step | 10M params Adam | p95 <= 130 ms |
| checkpoint save/load | 2 GB model state | throughput >= 350 MB/s |
| memory footprint | training step profile | peak RSS regression <= +8% |
| allocation churn | backward-heavy trace | alloc count regression <= +10% |
| tail stability | benchmark families | p99 regression <= +7% |

The spec's M4 milestone (Hardening) requires performance gates to pass. Currently we have exactly one benchmark (scalar DAC microbench, p95=4698ns) which doesn't cover any of the 8 SLOs.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a benchmark binary in ft-conformance (or a new crate) that runs each of the 8 workloads
2. For each: run 100 iterations, compute p50/p95/p99, compare against the budget
3. Emit structured JSON results to artifacts/phase2c/performance/
4. Create a gate script that reads the JSON and fails if any SLO is exceeded
5. Document whether FrankenTorch meets, exceeds, or fails each budget

Key consideration: We're CPU-only and pure Rust with no BLAS — we may not meet matmul or elementwise budgets for 100M elements. That's OK — the goal is to MEASURE and know where we stand, then document realistic budgets.

Tests:
- Benchmark binary should be runnable via: rch exec -- cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos
- JSON output should be machine-readable and diffable
- Gate script should exit 0 (pass) or 1 (fail) with human-readable report
close_reason: Implemented perf-SLO runner/gate, validated smoke path, and captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement with documented direct-budget failures plus baseline-required regression budgets; remaining baseline/CI handoff moved to frankentorch-3cp.

title: Add E2E training test for ConvTranspose1d
description:
Add a perfect-e2e regression for ConvTranspose1d (the recently-fixed zjf6 functional path's nn.Module twin). Trains a learnable transposed conv to map a 1x1x2 input up to 1x1x4, validating the scatter+pad+matmul backward path for 1D audio-style upsampling.

Setup:
- 1x1x2 fixed input
- ConvTranspose1d(1, 1, kernel=2, stride=2)
- Output: 1x1x4
- MSE against target
- Adam lr=0.05 for 200 steps
- Assert final loss < initial * 0.1

title: [perf] ft-serialize: single-pass validated f64 native save plan
description: Profile-backed follow-up after frankentorch-48yu rejected writer-capacity tuning and frankentorch-j125 already rejected the straightforward f64 chunk-buffer mirror. Fresh RCH Criterion baseline on worker vmi1149989 for native_state_dict/save_single_f64_1m: [932.39 us 988.10 us 1.0394 ms]. The f64 native save path is the largest remaining serializer row after f16/bf16/f32 SWAR packing. Hotspot hypothesis: native save performs a pre-write validation pass that calls contiguous_values for every f64 tensor and then the write pass calls contiguous_values again before emitting bytes; this repeats layout/storage checks and metadata traversal on the hot path. One lever only: build a validated native-save plan once after all fail-closed checks succeed, then write from that plan so each f64 tensor is validated/materialized once. Preserve BTreeMap order, dtype tags, shape/key bytes, pre-write fail-closed semantics, and exact f64 bit streams. Graveyard primitive: zero-copy typed framing plus cache-local planned execution; risk gate is constants-kill-you, so keep only if same-worker rebench scores >=2.0. Isomorphism proof: ordering/tie-breaking/RNG unaffected; floating-point values are never recomputed; golden f64 native-save sha256 must remain 106e45f354d304aba0ce939665820c20b312916d0ae193275a7e329ee3ce046e.
close_reason: Rejected: validated native-save plan preserved the f64 golden fixture but same-worker ts1 Criterion regressed native_state_dict/save_single_f64_1m from 944.80 us median to 976.52 us; source restored; evidence artifact recorded at artifacts/optimization/2026-06-05_ft_serialize_validated_save_plan_rejected_frankentorch-w0ez.md. Pivoting away from F64 save metadata/buffering variants.

title: [perf][ft-data] cache TensorDataset collation schema
description: Profile-backed target after frankentorch-javw rejection: RCH Criterion on vmi1149989 shows dataloader/epoch_2048x256_batch128 median 296.32 us. Graveyard primitive: cache/locality layout and invariant hoisting at the data-plane boundary. One lever: infer immutable homogeneous TensorDataset tensor schema at construction and route collate_indices through a fast path that skips repeated per-batch name/shape/value-length validation, falling back to the existing validator for heterogeneous or invalid datasets. Preserve batch order, tensor names/shapes, copied values, sampler RNG/order, and error behavior for invalid datasets; keep only if same-worker RCH rebench Score>=2.0 with golden sha256 unchanged.
close_reason: Rejected: TensorDataset schema-cache fast path regressed same-worker ts1 dataloader median 605.68 us -> 619.17 us. Source hunk manually removed; next ft-data pass should target a deeper contiguous/columnar layout primitive.

title: [parity-gap] Add nn.Unflatten module parity

Acceptance: add an ft-nn Unflatten module for integer dimensions, validate dim and unflattened_size product against the input dimension, compose through tensor_reshape for autograd, add forward/backward/error tests, and leave named-tensor string dims out of scope.

title: ft-api: f16/bf16 index_fill + index_put-overwrite (write-movement tail of u78p/0xe3)
description: Tail of the half write-movement vein. index_fill and index_put(accumulate=false) still hard-errored 'requires f32 or f64' for f16/bf16. Both are pure overwrite movement (no arithmetic) so bit-exact via the now-half scatter/index_put path. index_fill composes through tensor_scatter with a src_const cast to the input half dtype (rounds the scalar to half exactly as torch does). index_put accumulate=false gets a tape F16/BF16 arm guarded on !accumulate (upcast-f64 / index_put_tensor_contiguous_f64 / narrow); accumulate=true stays DEFERRED (arithmetic accumulation -> uv4l parity decision). Golden test f16_index_fill_index_put_preserve_dtype: exact == via f16/bf16 reps of 0.1/0.3, accumulate=true rejection, f32/f64 no-regression.
close_reason: SHIPPED e9b324a5. index_fill + index_put(accumulate=false) now support f16/bf16 bit-exactly via the half scatter/index_put path. Golden test f16_index_fill_index_put_preserve_dtype passes (exact == via half reps of 0.1/0.3, accumulate=true rejection, f32/f64 no-regression). ft-autograd 436 + ft-api 1914 lib tests pass. Half write-movement vein now COMPLETE: scatter/index_copy/put_along_dim (0xe3) + index_fill/index_put-overwrite (w5xi). Only ARITHMETIC-accumulation half ops remain (scatter_add/index_add/index_put-accumulate) -> uv4l, deferred pending torch f16 per-add rounding parity decision (torch not installed here to verify).

title: tensor_block_diag is severed-autograd: linear backward [child of d180/cross/i4rd cluster]
description:
tensor_block_diag at lib.rs:3852 places each input tensor as a block on the diagonal of a larger zero-initialized matrix. PyTorch's torch.block_diag is differentiable. Forward is purely linear in each input — output[row, col] = block_k[r, c] when (row, col) is in block k's region, 0 otherwise.

Backward extracts each block from the corresponding region of grad_output:
   grad_block_k[r, c] = grad_output[row_offset_k + r, col_offset_k + c]

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> backward that splits grad_output into per-input blocks. Shapes need to be reconstructed during backward; closure can capture (block_rows, block_cols, total_rows, total_cols) tuples.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_block_diag in tensor_apply_function with per-block extraction backward. Pre-computed (rows, cols, row_offset, col_offset, original_shape) for each input is captured by both the forward and backward closures so the same coordinate-mapping is used in both directions. Backward returns a Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> with one entry per input, each containing the corresponding sub-region of grad_output. Added block_diag_propagates_gradient_through_each_block regression test (two 2x2 blocks A=[[1,2],[3,4]], B=[[5,6],[7,8]] in a 4x4 output, weighted-sum loss with weights 0..15 → grad_a = [0,1,4,5], grad_b = [10,11,14,15]). ft-api lib tests: 1146.

title: ft-api: add tensor_masked_fill_ in-place op
close_reason: done

title: ft-api: linalg_qr autograd backward (two-node, tall reduced)
description: linalg_qr failed loud on requires_grad. QR VJP grad_A=(grad_Q+Q copyltu(M)) R^-T, M=R grad_R^T - grad_Q^T Q is linear in (grad_Q,grad_R), so split across two independent apply_function nodes (engine sums). Helper qr_backward_tall. Reduced QR, m>=n, full-rank R. FD-verified via linear loss <Q,Wq>+<R,Wr>; bit-exact value parity. frankentorch-rl4g.
close_reason: Shipped commit c8030699: linalg_qr autograd (tall reduced) via two-node split of the linear QR VJP, qr_backward_tall helper. FD-verified (linear loss) + bit-exact parity. Pushed main+master. Only linalg_svd remains.

title: [perf] ft-kernel-cpu: parallelize softmax/log_softmax dim family (left serial vs torch)
description: GAP: only f64 softmax last-dim fast path was parallel; f32 softmax fast path, BOTH log_softmax fast paths (cross-entropy/NLLLoss hot path), and ALL FOUR general strided paths ran serial — torch parallelizes softmax over any dim. LEVER: parallelize over independent output slices via par_chunks_mut (fast: chunks of reduce_size; general: per-outer contiguous chunks of reduce_size*inner_size with per-thread scratch). exp is compute-bound. ISOMORPHISM: each (outer,inner) slice computed identically (same gather/max/exp/pairwise-sum/scatter), chunk boundaries == slice boundaries -> bit-for-bit identical (proved softmax_family_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact, f64+f32, fast+strided, to_bits vs serial ref). BASELINE log_softmax f64 [8192,1024] dim1 = 152.55ms single-thread.
close_reason: Parallelized softmax/log_softmax dim family (f32 fast, both log_softmax fast, all 4 general strided); bit-exact (softmax_family_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact); log_softmax f64 3.3x, f32 2.25x, strided softmax 2.29x. 373 tests pass, clippy clean.

Test f32_pow_scalar_preserves_f32_dtype passes (f32[3] -> F32 + correct values + F64 unchanged); ft-api 1875 + ft-nn 708 green. Pushed main+master.

comments.author: RusticKite
comments.text: pow_scalar (ft-api ~54951) built a [1] exponent leaf via full() and called tensor_pow_tensor, which REQUIRES equal shapes (hard ShapeMismatch reject, no broadcast) -> pow_scalar ERRORED for any input not shaped [1]. It also materialized an F64 exponent that upcast f32 bases to F64. FIX: delegate to the canonical tensor_pow (dispatch_scalar_pow) which applies the scalar directly to the input tensor, preserving shape AND dtype. Found while sweeping weak-scalar siblings of the add/sub/mul/div_scalar fix (cijv).

title: porting: add tensor_amax / tensor_amin for torch.amax / torch.amin parity
description: torch.amax(input, dim) / torch.amin(input, dim) reduce along a dim and return ONLY values (no indices), unlike tensor_max_dim / tensor_min_dim which return (values, indices). Adding these as thin wrappers that discard the indices makes it easier to port torch code that uses the value-only interface (common in attention masking, beam search, etc.). Autograd-aware since the underlying max_dim/min_dim already route gradient through argmax/argmin.
close_reason: Added tensor_amax / tensor_amin in ft-api as thin wrappers over tensor_max_dim / tensor_min_dim that discard the index tensor. Matches torch.amax / torch.amin. Single-dim only for now (multi-dim is a follow-up). Autograd-aware via the underlying ops' argmax/argmin gradient routing. 2 unit tests cover forward correctness and gradient routing to the argmax position. ft-api now at 1236 lib tests.

title: sign returns -1.0 for -0.0, diverges from torch.sign
description: sign_scalar and sign_tensor_contiguous_f64 use Rust's f64::signum which returns -1.0 for -0.0 and 1.0 for 0.0 (sign-bit semantics). torch.sign returns 0.0 for both signed zeros. Existing tests at crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:9188-9206 document the Rust behavior as expected. Fix: replace .signum() with explicit branches that map both signed zeros to 0.0, propagate NaN, and return ±1.0 only for non-zero finite values. Update tests to assert torch parity.
close_reason: sign now matches torch.sign: both signed zeros map to +0.0, NaN propagates as NaN, and ±non-zero return ±1.0. F64 (sign_scalar/sign_tensor_contiguous_f64) and F32 (sign_tensor_contiguous_f32) all routed through new torch_sign_{f32,f64} helpers replacing Rust's IEEE-754-sign-bit signum. 4 new edge-case tests in ft-kernel-cpu + 2 stale ft-autograd tests fixed; ft-kernel-cpu 304 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-api 1086 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.

title: tensor_cummax / tensor_cummin sever autograd; argmax-routing backward needed
description:
tensor_cummax (lib.rs:10992) and tensor_cummin (lib.rs:11020) compute cumulative max/min along the flattened tensor and rebuild requires_grad=false leaves. PyTorch's torch.cummax / torch.cummin are differentiable.

Gradient flows back via argmax-routing: cum_max[i] takes its value from x[argmax_{j <= i} x[j]] = cum_idx[i]. So backward:
   grad_input[k] = sum over i where cum_idx[i] == k of grad_output[i]

The cum_idx tensor is already computed in the forward; saving it for backward avoids any extra work. Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this analytical backward. Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes:
Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_cummax and tensor_cummin's values branch in tensor_apply_function with argmax-routing backward: grad_input[k] = sum over i where cum_idx[i] == k of grad_output[i]. The cum_idx is computed in the forward (using a non-grad scan) and captured by the closure so the backward uses the same routing the forward used. The indices branch remains a non-grad leaf (correct — indices are integer-valued).

Added cummax_propagates_gradient_via_argmax_routing and cummin_propagates_gradient_via_argmin_routing regression tests asserting analytical gradients on representative input ([3,1,4,1,5] and [5,3,4,1,2]). ft-api lib tests: 1161.

title: ft-api: fix tensor_nll_loss / cross_entropy SEVERED gradient
description: tensor_nll_loss extracted values and rebuilt a requires_grad=false leaf, SILENTLY SEVERING the gradient of nll_loss, tensor_cross_entropy, and functional_cross_entropy (the most common training loss). Fixed to route through autograd-tracked tensor_gather (gather log_p at target, negate, reshape) while keeping reduction. Forward bit-identical; gradient now = softmax-onehot. Verified analytically. frankentorch-bug.
close_reason: Shipped commit 6875d8d3: fixed tensor_nll_loss severing autograd → cross_entropy/functional_cross_entropy now propagate gradient (softmax-onehot). Routed through tensor_gather, reduction preserved, forward bit-identical. Pushed main+master.

title: perfect-e2e: TripletMarginLoss metric learning training test
description: Add E2E perfect-e2e for a tiny metric-learning stack: a shared Linear embedding net mapping 3 inputs (anchor / positive / negative) into a small embedding space, with TripletMarginLoss. Trains with Adam to drive the loss to (or close to) zero — i.e. the network learns to push negatives outside the margin while keeping positives close. Locks the gradient flow through the triplet-margin composition (sub + mul + sum + sqrt + relu) and surfaces any regression in metric-learning workflows.
close_reason: Added triplet_margin_metric_learning_trains_end_to_end_with_adam — Linear(4→4) shared embedding net, 3 fixed inputs (anchor/positive/negative), TripletMarginLoss(margin=1, p=2). 200 Adam steps; expects 10x loss reduction. Locks the autograd chain sub → mul → sum → sqrt → relu inside the triplet loss. ft-conformance now at 147 base lib tests, 191 with --features fuzz.

title: EvidenceLedger.entries grows unbounded — inference memory leak
description: ft-runtime EvidenceLedger.record() (crates/ft-runtime/src/lib.rs:39) pushes one heap-allocated EvidenceEntry{ts_unix_ms, kind, summary:String} per op into an unbounded Vec, via all 203 record_tensor_* callers in ft-api. No cap, no ring buffer, no truncate/clear/retain/drain, and NO public clear() API (only entries()/len()/is_empty()). A long-running session or inference server grows this Vec forever (~150 bytes + a String alloc per op) and cannot reclaim it short of dropping the whole RuntimeContext. Fix options: (a) bounded ring buffer keeping last N entries; (b) add a clear()/truncate API consumers can call; (c) gate recording behind a debug/strict flag so release/inference paths skip it. Tests read entries() (e.g. runtime_policy_evidence), so preserve read semantics; cap/clear, don't remove. Verified 2026-06-04.
close_reason: Fixed in 2f820623: SOFT_CAP(32768) bound on EvidenceLedger.record() draining oldest-middle entries (keeps policy anchor + recent tail), plus clear() API. New test locks bounded behavior. All ft-runtime tests pass.

FrankenTorch ft-api already has tensor_interpolate for these modes, and ft-nn has specialized Upsample1d/2d/3d plus PyTorch-named 2D wrappers, but no generic nn.Upsample module. Acceptance: add Upsample in ft-nn with size or scale_factor targets, default nearest mode, optional align_corners, scalar/per-axis constructors, no parameters, validation for mutually-exclusive/missing target, shape/value tests for nearest and bilinear, gradient test, and validate via rch ft-nn gates plus UBS.

title: [perf] ft-optim: fuse AdamW first-step moment initialization
description: GAP: AdamW first-step bench stays at p50 428.82 us for 64x1024 params; current first-step path allocates/fills next_m and next_v in separate passes, then walks them again to update params. TARGET: reduce optimizer step-time tail toward torch optimizer throughput without changing AdamW math. BASELINE: rch exec -- cargo bench -p ft-optim --bench optimizer_bench -- adamw/step_64x1024 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 5 --sample-size 20 on vmi1293453 = [420.57 us 428.82 us 435.49 us]. LEVER: first-step only (m/v None) fused moment-buffer construction plus param update in one per-element pass, storing state only after session update succeeds. ALIEN primitive: vectorized/morsel loop fusion and allocation-pressure elimination; fallback is keep existing multi-pass path. PROOF: bit-exact AdamW first-step golden output + unchanged ordering/errors/RNG/tie-breaking; rebench via rch and keep only Score>=2.0.

title: Escalate blocked_issues_cache import-path page leak and preserve safe tracker workflow
description:
Follow-up to frankentorch-xsp.

Investigation isolated the integrity regression to the import path: explicit `br sync --import-only` and any command that auto-imports current JSONL into a clean vacuumed DB. The stable signature is blocked_issues_cache churn: old blocked-cache table/index pages become `PRAGMA integrity_check` `never used` pages after import/rebuild.

Safe on a clean vacuumed DB: `br info`, `br doctor`, `br sync --status`, and commands with `--no-auto-import`.

Artifacts:
- artifacts/beads_recovery/FRANKENTORCH_XSP_IMPORT_PATH_REPORT_20260407.md
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_probe_20260407T025948Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_import_focus_20260407T030054Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_repeat_import_20260407T030112Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_sequence_20260407T030136Z/results.json

Next work:
1. Escalate the import-path / blocked-cache page leak upstream in beads_rust or frankensqlite.
2. Preserve a local recovery playbook that warns agents to avoid auto-import on freshly vacuumed recovery DBs until the upstream fix lands.
3. Re-validate the workaround after the upstream fix by repeating the xsp probe matrix.
close_reason: Filed upstream escalation as beads_rust#224, expanded the archived import-path report with a concrete local recovery playbook, and added a temporary AGENTS.md warning to keep vacuumed recovery DB sessions on --no-auto-import until the fix lands.

title: [perf] ft-optim: fuse AdamW moment/update pass
description: Profile-backed target after ready queue drained: rch Criterion adamw/step_64x1024 baseline [334.37 us 339.78 us 344.65 us] from cargo bench -p ft-optim --bench optimizer_bench -- adamw/step_64x1024. Hotspot: AdamW step currently builds next_m and next_v in separate iterator passes, then walks params again for the update. One lever: after the same state/shape validations and bias-correction computation, fuse per-element next_m/next_v construction with local param update while preserving the exact arithmetic order for m, v, m_hat, v_hat, adam_delta, weight-decay delta, and session/state commit ordering. Isomorphism: no ordering/tie/RNG changes; floating-point operations per element unchanged; optimizer state and parameter values must match golden sha256 bit-for-bit. Keep only if rebench score >=2.0.
close_reason: Already implemented: AdamW step is single-pass fused (compute m/v + param update + state collection in one closure pass; bias-correction hoisted). The bead described the pre-fuse state. Remaining next_m/next_v alloc is intentional for fail-closed state-commit semantics; removing it would violate that contract and is sub-2.0 anyway.

comments.author: cc_1
comments.text: ALREADY FUSED in current tree (ft-optim/src/lib.rs ~689-769). The single tensor_update_param_values_f64_with closure computes m_val, v_val, m_hat, v_hat, adam_delta, decay_delta and writes *p in ONE pass over the elements — there is no separate next_m/next_v build pass and no second param walk (the state the bead described). Bias-correction is hoisted once (657-658). The only remaining separate step is pushing to next_m/next_v before committing self.m[i]/self.v[i]; that is INTENTIONAL to preserve the documented fail-closed 'commit state only after the param write succeeds' semantics — mutating m/v in place would corrupt optimizer state if the param write errors mid-loop. No >=2.0 lever remains; the described fuse is done.

title: tensor_diff F32 path silently severs autograd; tensor_sub doesn't preserve F32
description:
tensor_diff at lib.rs:10919 (F32 branch) extracted values, computed differences in plain f32, and rebuilt a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd. The F64 path was already correctly composed through tensor_narrow + tensor_sub.

Attempted to compose F32 the same way, but ran into 'UnsupportedDType(F64)' because tensor_sub on F32 inputs appears to silently upcast (or fall back to an F64-only kernel) and produces an F64 output, breaking the F32 dtype contract.

Interim fix: add an explicit fail-loud guard on requires_grad=true for the F32 path so autograd severance is no longer silent. Long-term fix: make tensor_sub (and other binary ops) dtype-preserving on F32, then unify the F32 path with the F64 narrow + sub composition.

Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.
notes: Partial fix in this commit: F32 + requires_grad now fails-loud instead of silently severing. The proper composable F32 path is blocked by tensor_sub upcasting F32 inputs (UnsupportedDType(F64) error when we tried to compose narrow + sub on F32 input). Long-term fix tracked in this bead — needs dtype-preserving F32 binary ops first.

title: multi_margin_loss severs autograd; composable through gather/relu/pow/mask
description:
multi_margin_loss at lib.rs:10450 computes per-sample sum_{j != y} relu(margin - input[i,y] + input[i,j])^p / classes, accumulates in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd for multi-class margin training paths.

PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.multi_margin_loss IS differentiable (sub-gradient at the relu hinge). Compose through autograd primitives:
  mask_y = one_hot(target, classes)                                  # [batch, classes]
  mask_others = 1 - mask_y
  score_y = sum(input * mask_y, dim=1, keepdim=True)                 # [batch, 1]
  score_y_b = expand to [batch, classes]
  diff = (margin scalar) + input - score_y_b
  per_elem = relu(diff) * mask_others                                # zero at j == y
  per_elem_p = per_elem^p
  loss = mean(sum(per_elem_p, dim=1)) / classes

Sister of the recent loss-severance cluster (ilwr / 4liu / tr2a). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.
notes: Fixed in this commit. Composed multi_margin_loss through one_hot + relu + pow + sum_dim + mean primitives. The mask_others = 1 - one_hot(target) handles the j != y exclusion via element-wise multiplication. score_y is gathered via input * mask_y then summed (instead of integer indexing). The forward shape contract is preserved; existing value tests continue to pass. Added multi_margin_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (input=[[0,3]], target=[0], p=1, margin=1 → analytical [-0.5, 0.5]). ft-api lib tests: 1154.

title: conv2d no-grad F64 routes through fused kernel (was serial im2col gather) — ~2.8x
description: functional_conv2d's no-grad path did a SERIAL 6-deep manual im2col gather + tensor_matmul (materialising weight^T), while the grad path used the fused parallel conv2d_forward_f64 (par_chunks im2col + dgemm_bt + parallel reorg). So conv2d inference (the common case) was ~2.5-2.8x slower than it needed to be — and ~8x slower than torch single-thread on the ops_bench (hw/128 589ms). Fix: F64 no-grad now calls conv2d_forward_f64 directly (leaf, no tape). Same-worker criterion A/B: conv2d/hw/32 33.5->13.5ms (-56%), hw/64 130->49ms (-62%), hw/128 589->207ms (-65%, 2.85x). Bit-exact (the existing functional_conv2d_no_grad_fast_path_matches_composed_path_bit_exact test proves old serial-gather == conv2d_forward_f64, so new == old to the bit); 1860 ft-api + 708 ft-nn tests pass. F32 keeps the composed path.
close_reason: Shipped f25b0550: conv2d no-grad F64 routed through fused conv2d_forward_f64 (was serial 6-deep im2col gather + tensor_matmul). Same-worker criterion A/B: hw/32 33.5->13.5ms, hw/64 130->49ms, hw/128 589->207ms (2.85x, grows with size). Bit-exact to old (existing no_grad==grad bit-exact test passes); 1860 ft-api + 708 ft-nn tests pass. Pushed main+master.

title: [perf] ft-kernel-cpu: parallelize sort-along-dim over lanes (serial vs torch)
description: GAP: sort_tensor_contiguous_f64/f32 sorted each (outer,inner) lane in a serial double loop; torch sorts lanes in parallel. Sorting is compute-bound O(dim*log dim) per lane. LEVER: parallelize over per-outer contiguous output blocks (dim_size*inner_size) via par_chunks_mut on sorted_values+indices zipped with input; each lane sorted independently with the same stable sort + fixed nan_greatest comparator. ISOMORPHISM: bit-identical values AND indices (proved sort_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact, strided+last-dim, with duplicates to exercise tie-break). BENCH (RAYON_NUM_THREADS=1 vs all cores, [8192,1024] dim1): 365.9->101.7ms (3.60x). NOTE: also investigated parallelizing LU/det trailing update — REVERTED, it is a bandwidth-bound rank-1 update + n fork/join barriers, no speedup (needs blocked/GEMM LU which is not bit-exact).
close_reason: Parallelized sort-along-dim over lanes (f64+f32); bit-exact values+indices (sort_parallel_matches_serial_bit_exact); [8192,1024] 365.9->101.7ms 3.60x. 377 tests pass, clippy clean. LU trailing-update parallelization investigated+reverted (bandwidth-bound, no win).

title: [perf] ft-api: cache sinusoidal PE denominators
description: Profile-backed target after ready queue drained and claimed perf beads avoided. Baseline: rch Criterion on ts1, cargo bench -p ft-api --bench ops_bench -- sinusoidal_pe/4096x1024 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 5 --sample-size 20, time [3.1785 ms 3.6985 ms 4.3931 ms] with high outliers. Current sinusoidal_positional_encoding recomputes 10000.powf((2*i)/d_model) for every (pos,i), even though the denominator depends only on i. Golden sha256 and bit-exact test required. Keep only if rebench Score >=2.0.

title: Repair beads SQLite integrity drift after doctor-repair
description:
## Context
`br doctor --repair` recovered ready-state correctness, but it still reports `sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`. JSONL is canonical and `br ready --json` is already correct, so the remaining work is to rebuild the SQLite side cleanly from JSONL and verify the tracker is healthy.

## Goal
Restore `.beads` to a clean, non-stale state with `br doctor` green except for any known frankensqlite-only sidecar warning.

## Acceptance
- `br ready --json` remains stable
- `br doctor` no longer reports `db.recoverable_anomalies` or stale ready-state drift
- If possible, clear the SQLite malformed-page warning by rebuilding DB from JSONL
- Document any residual unavoidable warning in the close reason
close_reason: Snapshotted .beads, rebuilt an isolated DB from canonical JSONL, promoted it over the stale canonical DB, and verified br ready is empty plus br doctor cleared recoverable anomalies; residual warnings are limited to preserved backup artifacts, frankensqlite WAL sidecar behavior, missing sqlite3, and a page-17 integrity warning that persists even after a fresh rebuild

title: Investigate beads/frankensqlite page-reuse integrity warning after writes
description:
## Context
`frankentorch-c84` diagnosed the residual `br doctor` / `PRAGMA integrity_check` warning in the FrankenTorch tracker.

## Findings
- live `.beads/beads.db` remained operational and in sync with `.beads/issues.jsonl`
- an isolated rebuild from canonical JSONL (`.beads/beads.db.rebuild_c84_20260407T013146Z`) reproduced the `Page N: never used` integrity warning
- a `VACUUM INTO` rewrite (`.beads/beads.db.vacuum_c84_20260407T013244Z`) passed `integrity_check`, `quick_check`, and `br doctor --db ...`
- after promoting the vacuumed copy to canonical `.beads/beads.db`, subsequent normal `br` operations reintroduced the warning (`page 19 is never used`)

## Interpretation
This looks like a `beads_rust` / frankensqlite writer bug or page-accounting bug, not JSONL drift and not unrecoverable logical data corruption.

## Artifacts
- `artifacts/beads_recovery/c84_snapshot_20260407T013033Z/`
- `.beads/beads.db.rebuild_c84_20260407T013146Z`
- `.beads/beads.db.vacuum_c84_20260407T013244Z`
- `.beads/beads.db.bad_20260407T013409Z`
- `artifacts/beads_recovery/c84_checkpoint_test_20260407T013229Z.db`

## Next step
Trace which write path (export, write_probe, cache rebuild, or WAL handling) is reintroducing unreachable pages after a clean vacuum.
close_reason: Investigated and isolated to import-only / auto-import blocked_issues_cache rebuild path; follow-up tracked separately

title: Add fail-loud guards for FFT ops with requires_grad
description:
tensor_fft, tensor_ifft, tensor_rfft, tensor_irfft all extract tensor_values from input, compute the DFT in plain f64, and rebuild non-grad real/imag leaves before tensor_complex. While tensor_complex itself fails loud when its inputs have requires_grad=true, the silent severance happens upstream: with requires_grad input, the tensor_values extraction strips the autograd tape, then re/im non-grad rebuilds satisfy the tensor_complex check, and the user gets a complex output with no gradient back to input.

Add fail-loud guards on all four FFT ops when input requires_grad. This matches the pattern used for stft (frankentorch-3v6e), where Wirtinger calculus would be needed for proper backward.

Affects (all in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs):
- tensor_fft (line 15064)
- tensor_ifft (line 15105)
- tensor_rfft (line 15161)
- tensor_irfft (line 15200)

title: [perf][no-gaps] ft-api: exploit symmetric SupCon similarity matrix
description: Profile-backed fallback target after br ready --json returned [] and existing perf beads were assigned to ft-nn, ft-kernel-cpu GEMM, and ft-optim. Fresh rch baseline on ts1: cargo bench -p ft-api --bench ops_bench -- supcon_loss/512x512 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 5 --sample-size 20 => [9.0834 ms 9.4931 ms 10.127 ms]. Current SupCon computes the full N x N normalized dot-product matrix even though sim(i,j)=sim(j,i). One lever: compute only the upper-triangle dot products in row order, mirror the lower triangle after all dot rows complete, and leave loss row traversal, max/log-sum-exp, positive handling, label casting, shape/errors, RNG, and temperature scaling unchanged. Proof obligations: bit-exact scalar loss vs full-matrix serial reference, identical dot k-summation order, multiplication operand commutativity documented, golden sha256, rch rebench Score >= 2.0.

title: EmbeddingBag.forward_with_offsets severs autograd
description:
EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets in ft-nn (line 2366) extracts tensor_values from indices, offsets, weight, and per_sample_weights, computes the bag aggregation in plain f64, and rebuilds via session.tensor_variable(output, vec![num_bags, dim], false). This silently severs autograd through the embedding weight AND through per_sample_weights — breaking training for any model using nn.EmbeddingBag (NLP classification, recommendation systems, fastText-style models).

Fix: compose via tensor_index_select + tensor_scatter_add (or matmul with a precomputed indicator matrix). Strategy:
1. Look up rows: emb = tensor_index_select(weight, 0, indices) → [N, D]
2. If per_sample_weights: emb = emb * psw_broadcast (autograd-aware)
3. Per-bag aggregation:
   - Build a [N] non-grad bag_assignment tensor: bag_assignment[i] = which bag i belongs to
   - For Sum: scatter_add into [num_bags, D] zeros. Mean: divide by bag_size after.
   - For Max: needs different routing (gather max per bag) — defer or composed via softmax-based approximation.
4. Handle padding_idx by zeroing out those rows pre-aggregation via mask.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs EmbeddingBag::forward_with_offsets (line 2366).

title: [parity-gap] Add nn.CircularPad3d module parity
